kept.text: @PopCrave @RokhaLovesHS Fuck it let me stream some sombr

Last week I sat down with Zach Abrams, co-founder of Bridge, which was acquire by Stripe for $1.1 billion in October 2024. 

At the time, it was the largest crypto acquisition ever, and plenty of people thought they overpaid. But now a competitor sold for $1.8B. 

So does he think he sold too early? 

(0:43) Does Zach Abrams Regret Selling to Stripe?
(2:20) How Did Stripe Master Customer Cohorts?
(5:23) How Do You Scale From $1M to $10M in Revenue? 
(7:58) John vs. Patrick Collison: Who is Smarter? 
(10:44) What Changed After the Acquisition? 
(14:24) How Does an Acquisition Impact Inbound? 
(15:29) Why is Open Stablecoin Issuance Critical? 
(18:18) Why is Klarna Building Its Own Stablecoin? 
(22:16) How Did Chipper Cash Bypass SWIFT Payments? 
(28:17) Why Are Enterprises Moving to Stablecoins? 
(33:36) Do Stablecoins Have US Product-Market Fit? 
(35:54) Will Non-USD Stablecoins Dominate the Market?

(41:25) Where is the Real Stablecoin Volume Going? 
(44:27) Are AI Agents the Future of Money? 
(47:07) Can You Access APIs Without an API Key? 
(48:48) Rapid Fire: SF, Claude Code & Token Maxing

kept.text:
AI is quietly reshaping who “wins” in finance

It’s not about market share.
It’s about AI visibility.

Leaders by category:

Cards: Capital One dominates recall
BNPL: Klarna owns the narrative (91%)
Wallets: Apple (84%) interface control
P2P: PayPal still default (85%)
Crypto: Coinbase (84%) entry point

Key insight:
AI doesn’t reward depth, it rewards default answers.

Implication:
If your brand isn’t what AI returns…
you don’t exist at the decision layer.

Next battleground:
Docs, APIs, content, data.

Distribution is shifting:
from search results to model outputs.
